files.keys_format: TripleKey[8631890] where TripleKey={uint32_t a, uint32_t b, uint32_t c}

storage_extensions.usage: mmap offsets.bin and keys.bin; compute partition = hash_triple(adsh, tag, version) >> 45, then linearly scan keys[offsets[partition] .. offsets[partition + 1]) for an exact anti-join existence check

join_order.probe: num(filtered: ddate range via merged postings, uom=USD via dense column, value IS NOT NULL)
join_order.notes: Keep the existing cache-friendly pre partitioned exact index, but feed it from a globally rowid-ordered 2023 candidate stream produced by merging the 12 date postings runs instead of probing from date-group order with a 33.2M-row USD bitset.

scan.access_pattern: Use num_ddate_postings as 12 sorted rowid runs for the 2023 dates, k-way merge them into one globally ascending rowid stream, then check num/uom.bin directly for USD and read value/adsh/tag/version in the same rowid order so dense num columns are consumed almost sequentially before probing pre.

data_structures.dim_filter: 12-way min_heap or loser_tree over num_ddate_postings rowid runs plus direct dense equality on num/uom.bin
data_structures.fact_join: pre_built_index(hash_partitioned_exact_triples_p19) using uint64_t partition offsets plus contiguous TripleKey slices

indexes.purpose: enumerate 2023 candidates as 12 sorted rowid runs that can be merged into global rowid order
exact anti-join membership on (adsh, tag, version) with tiny per-partition scans

optimization_notes: Root cause: the current plan chooses the 2023 date postings as the driver but then materializes a 33.2M-row USD membership bitset and processes the 12 date posting groups in date-value order, which turns the hot path into partially random gathers across num columns. Strategy category A: restructure the scan so the 12 date posting runs are k-way merged into one globally rowid-ordered stream, test uom by directly reading the 75 MB num/uom.bin column instead of building a postings bitset, and keep the existing partitioned exact pre anti-join plus thread-local (tag,version) aggregation. This removes the expensive dim_filter build and converts the surviving column reads into near-sequential bandwidth-friendly access.
